justification: The diff comprehensively migrates javax.* to jakarta.* and updates Swagger annotations to OpenAPI 3 (@Schema, @Tag, @Operation), which is excellent antipattern clearance. It also adopts Java 21 idioms like text blocks (LimitAspect.java), String.formatted() (CustomP6SpyLogger.java), pattern matching for instanceof (BigDecimalUtils.java, FileUtil.java, RedisUtils.java, GlobalExceptionHandler.java), and List.getFirst() (AnonTagUtils.java, GeneratorServiceImpl.java, QueryHelp.java). However, the SwaggerConfig.java change removes @EnableSwagger2 without adding @EnableOpenApi, which might break Swagger UI functionality unless handled elsewhere, slightly reducing coherence. Also, some imports are reordered or cleaned up (e.g., PageUtil.java, ColUtil.java), which is good but minor.
